scope: User-requested top-100 calibration review after late research batches pushed several mid-major/role-only players too high and held multiple high-profile prospects too low.
method: Rechecked every user-named player against current projection inputs, roster context, production signal, recruiting/international profile, and shared calibration formulas.
Lowered speculative top-100 scores where a role-only or mid-major profile had been given too strong a synthetic historical anchor.
Raised high-major proven/talent profiles whose current trajectory was not reflected by stale or missing anchors.
Projected Mark Padjen directly from official Murray State/FIBA context and the same international newcomer calibration.
Re-ranked the full projected pool after applying the same projectedBbpr calculation path.

players.reason: Momcilovic was too low because the old projection treated Kentucky opportunity as a low-change role. The correction reflects a proven high-major scoring forward with clear Kentucky spacing and usage value.

evidence.notes: Official Kentucky roster context verifies current-team fit for Momcilovic.

players.reason: Johnson was low because the old row had bench-level opportunity and talent inputs despite a strong historical anchor. The correction reflects a real Iowa State role and high-major tools without forcing him into the national top-25 tier.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Johnson in Iowa State's current player pool.

players.reason: Mullins still carried a freshman-reserve anchor even though his current Connecticut projection uses elite sophomore role and talent inputs. The score now uses the same breakout-returner weighting as similar players.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Mullins with Connecticut.

players.reason: Bradshaw was too low for a former blue-chip big now in a larger Tennessee State role, though conference context keeps him below the high-major top-100 locks.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Bradshaw with Tennessee State.

players.reason: Lewis had top-100 role inputs but a stale Villanova anchor. The correction sets a comparable synthetic sophomore-transfer anchor before applying the same transfer formula.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Lewis with Miami.

players.reason: Tobiason had a transfer-anchor warning because the model inputs were stronger than the old anchor. The correction gives him a fair Syracuse role anchor without forcing top-100 status.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Tobiason with Syracuse.

players.reason: Folgueiras was just below the top-100 line despite a productive forward profile and Louisville frontcourt opportunity. The corrected anchor keeps him in the high-major starter band.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Folgueiras with Louisville.

players.reason: Allen needed more credit for a sophomore SEC trajectory, positional size, and NBA-facing tools. Alabama depth still keeps him below the elite proven tier.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Allen with Alabama.

players.reason: Stojakovic was outside the top 100 because his historical anchor was far too low for his current Illinois scoring trajectory and NBA-facing wing profile.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Stojakovic with Illinois.

players.reason: Page was too low for a former high-major frontcourt prospect, but the Providence role is still less certain than the top-100 frontcourt group.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Page with Providence.

players.reason: Oladotun was the kept Maryland freshman row and sat one hundredth below the top-100 line. The correction gives the actual Babatunde row the same elite-freshman treatment while preserving newcomer compression.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Oladotun with Maryland.

players.reason: Dak was a bit low for a high-major transfer frontcourt role, but the corrected score keeps him around the top-100 bubble rather than a locked top-75 player.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Dak with Vanderbilt.

players.reason: Ndongo was low for a productive high-major frontcourt transfer. The correction gives his Pittsburgh role a real starter anchor while keeping the NBA/tools score moderate.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Ndongo with Pittsburgh.

players.reason: Fland was too low because the historical anchor did not reflect a top high-major guard talent with Florida opportunity. The correction moves him into top-100 range without treating him as proven top-25 production.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Fland with Florida.

players.reason: Benjamin's row had a high projection model against a middling transfer anchor. The correction normalizes him as a useful Boston College forward without top-100 certainty.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Benjamin with Boston College.

players.reason: Johnson's Denver row had elite-usage inputs that overstated the national top-100 translation. The correction keeps the scorer profile but removes the top-100 role flag.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Johnson with Denver.

players.reason: Carr was far too low for a Michigan State returning athlete with top-100 tools and a real frontcourt role, even with a controlled offensive burden.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Carr with Michigan State.

players.reason: Chandler was too low for a former high-profile guard now in a BYU role path, but the update stops short of top-100 certainty because his role remains less bankable.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Chandler with BYU.

players.reason: Wagner was wildly low because his Maryland transfer row carried an extremely stale anchor. The correction restores a former elite recruit/high-major guard profile into the top-100 range.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Wagner with Maryland.

players.reason: Ames needed a higher Tennessee-transfer anchor because the current row showed starter-level SEC opportunity but retained an old low historical base.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Ames with Tennessee.

players.reason: Punch was a bit low relative to his Texas frontcourt role, Big 12 production, and defensive value. The correction moves him into a stronger high-major starter band.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Punch's Texas fit.

players.reason: Vanterpool had the same stale-anchor pattern as the other Providence transfers, but not enough certainty for top-100 treatment. The correction moves the anchor into a consistent high-major reserve-to-starter band.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Vanterpool with Providence.

players.reason: Shelstad was massively low because an Oregon-to-Louisville transfer row carried a stale guard anchor. The correction reflects a proven high-major creator with a clear Louisville perimeter role.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Shelstad with Louisville.

players.reason: Haralson was being dragged by a low Notre Dame freshman anchor. The correction treats him as a blue-chip sophomore transfer with Tennessee role upside, not as a settled low-usage reserve.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Haralson with Tennessee.

players.reason: Foxwell's Arizona State row was over-signaling opportunity relative to the anchor. The correction leaves him in the watch-list range rather than a hidden top-100 lock.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Foxwell with Arizona State.

players.reason: Mobley was top-100 but still suppressed by a low historical anchor relative to his Ohio State scoring role and efficiency trajectory.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Mobley with Ohio State.

players.reason: Tugler should not sit outside the top 100 given Houston's defensive identity and his proven frontcourt impact. Offensive burden is still not treated like a scoring centerpiece.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Tugler's current Houston role environment.

players.reason: Hubbard was the clearest stale-anchor miss: his Mississippi State star usage and production were sitting outside the top 100. The correction moves him into the national star tier.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Hubbard with Mississippi State.

players.reason: Pippen was low for an Ohio State developmental guard profile with starter-level inputs. The adjustment adds a fair sophomore-transfer anchor without pushing him past proven stars.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Pippen with Ohio State.

players.reason: Catchings was low for a former high-profile wing with a Georgia opportunity path. The correction lifts the anchor while leaving him behind more proven top-100 locks.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Catchings with Georgia.

players.reason: Mingo was held down by a stale Penn State freshman anchor despite a Baylor guard role and sophomore development profile.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Mingo with Baylor.

players.reason: Overton was below the line because of a low anchor despite a credible Auburn rotation-to-starter path. The correction places him on the top-100 bubble.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Overton with Auburn.

players.reason: Mack was severely low because the transfer row kept an old anchor despite a Providence usage profile that should rate near the top-100 line.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Mack with Providence.

players.reason: Vokietaitis was low for a productive, efficient center with Texas frontcourt value. The correction raises role and tools while stopping short of elite offensive burden.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Vokietaitis with Texas.

players.reason: Byrd was too low for a proven high-major transfer wing, though Providence role uncertainty keeps him outside the safer top-100 band.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Byrd with Providence.

players.reason: Avdalas was far too low for a high-profile North Carolina international/transfer profile. The corrected synthetic anchor reflects international production and NBA-facing tools before the standard transfer blend.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Avdalas with North Carolina.
FIBA profile context supports the international production signal used for the synthetic anchor.

players.reason: Freeman was one of the biggest misses: a productive high-major big transferred to Auburn but retained a low stale anchor. The correction places him with other proven top-100 frontcourt players.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Freeman with Auburn.

players.reason: Hinton's old inputs gave him a top-100 role signal without a top-100 anchor. The correction keeps him as a strong Harvard scorer while removing the high-major top-100 role equivalency.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Hinton with Harvard.

players.reason: Wright was already high, but his elite BYU role inputs were still slightly compressed by an old anchor. The correction raises the anchor without moving him above the proven All-American tier.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Wright with BYU.

players.reason: Rice was too low for a USC guard role with a solid high-major scoring profile. The correction puts him near the back of the top-100 conversation.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Rice with USC.

players.reason: Henderson was just under the top-100 line with Miami role/talent inputs that matched other corrected high-major sophomore profiles. The anchor is lifted enough to remove the stale freshman drag without overrating the role certainty.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Henderson with Miami.

players.reason: Cyril was too low for Miami's rim-protection need and his high-major center trajectory. The adjustment lifts the tools and role while keeping offensive burden controlled.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Cyril with Miami.

players.reason: Pettiford was much too low because his Auburn returner row had a freshman anchor that did not match his expected expanded role and NBA-facing guard tools.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Pettiford with Auburn.

players.reason: Buljan was high for a crowded Maryland frontcourt profile. The new score keeps the international-production anchor but removes top-100 role certainty.

evidence.notes: Official roster context shows Maryland frontcourt competition around Buljan.

players.reason: Ivisic was low for a proven Illinois frontcourt piece with production, size, and NBA-facing tools. The update moves him back into the likely top-100 range.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Ivisic with Illinois.

players.reason: Edmonds was low for a TCU frontcourt starter-quality profile. The correction raises anchor and tools without making him a primary scorer.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Edmonds with TCU.

players.reason: Bynum was low after a productive freshman season with a large start count. The correction gives him a stronger sophomore frontcourt trajectory while preserving development uncertainty.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Bynum with Ohio State.

players.reason: Nwoko was low for a proven high-major center moving into a clear Xavier frontcourt role. The correction lifts the anchor and tools while keeping creation modest.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Nwoko with Xavier.

players.reason: Padjen was blank despite Murray State signing context, Slovenian senior-team involvement, professional-club production, and FIBA U20 MVP momentum. He belongs in the back-end top-100 mix after international newcomer compression.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Padjen with Murray State.
FIBA named Padjen U20 EuroBasket MVP after leading Slovenia to the title.

players.reason: Pate was sitting one spot outside the top 100 despite a pro-track newcomer profile and Providence creation opportunity. The correction nudges role certainty while still applying the freshman cap.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Pate with Providence.

players.reason: Sanders was high because a young Air Force guard role was weighted like a top-100 profile. The correction keeps developmental value but removes the inflated offensive role.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Sanders with Air Force.

players.reason: Saran was high for a Richmond guard profile. The correction still values role and production but moves him below the national top-100 band.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Saran with Richmond.

players.reason: Carson Schwieger was high because the Tulane role was treated like a top-100 lock. The corrected score keeps him above the broad cutoff but out of the top 100.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Schwieger with Tulane.

players.reason: Cooper Schwieger was high for a Florida State frontcourt transfer whose role is good but not top-70 certain. The correction lowers role and anchor.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Schwieger with Florida State.

players.reason: Simpkins was high after receiving top-100 guard role inputs. The correction keeps a useful Mississippi State projection but removes the inflated high-major burden.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Simpkins with Mississippi State.

players.reason: Smith was high because an excellent college guard anchor combined with too much Notre Dame certainty. He remains top-100 caliber, but no longer a top-40 player.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Smith with Notre Dame.

players.reason: Stromer was high because Gonzaga pedigree and San Diego opportunity were over-weighted. The correction values shooting experience but moves him out of top-100 range.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Stromer with San Diego.

players.reason: Taylor was wildly high because a strong Oakland on-ball role was treated like a national top-40 anchor. The correction keeps him as a good college guard but outside the top 100.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Taylor with Oakland.

players.reason: Tillery was high for a young Charlotte guard with development value but not enough top-100 certainty. The correction keeps him above 60 and away from the top-100 line.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Tillery with Charlotte.

players.reason: Traore was high after receiving too much West Virginia certainty. The correction keeps a useful veteran-forward projection but removes top-70 placement.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Traore with West Virginia.

players.reason: Webber was high because his sophomore development path was too aggressively converted into top-100 value. The correction still credits center size and role.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Webber with Southern Illinois.

players.reason: White was way too high for an Akron transfer profile and should not be sitting near national top-50 names. The correction lowers anchor, role, and confidence.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies White with Akron.

players.reason: White was high after receiving too much Temple backcourt certainty. The correction keeps him above 60 without top-100 placement.

evidence.notes: Official Temple roster context verifies White's current team and transfer path.

players.reason: Willis was high for a Kent State guard profile. The correction keeps strong college-guard value but moves him well below the top-100 line.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Willis with Kent State.

players.reason: Boumtje Boumtje was too low after the newcomer cap suppressed an elite Duke freshman profile. The correction raises role and top-end tools while still applying freshman compression.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Boumtje Boumtje with Duke.
USA Basketball profile lists Boumtje Boumtje's Duke commitment context.
